$comment: D-AS-REGALLOC-ARG-REGISTER-OCCUPIED (c75 correctness fix, variant 2 — FP). The FP sibling of arg_reg_gpr_pressure. Under FP register pressure the allocator assigned an incoming FP argument register (x86_64 SysV xmm7 = the 8th FP arg register) to a DIFFERENT vreg's home while the incoming param was still live in it -> the param read as a neighbour -> 54 instead of 42 (a SILENT miscompile; a PRE-EXISTING allocator bug that c75's added pressure unmasked). FIX (lir_regalloc.cpp): collectArgRegisterOccupied + a covered-window excludedScratch augment models each incoming arg register as occupied over [entry, argOp.late) and keeps allocation (and spill-evict) off it while a range that could alias the incoming param is live. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the fix -> x86_64 returns 54 (WRONG). arm64 (32 FPRs) never spills here (trivially correct, value-checked). fsum = 2 * (1+2+3+4+5+6) = 42.0 -> (int)42.
